mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of instances where unemployment crosses the 5.5 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer spending strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5 percent over 60-day holding periods in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during confirmed economic expansions reflects accelerating factory output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Short-term returns turn negative in more than 55 percent of expansion-phase months with rising industrial production.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index reading above 3.0 percent signals persistent inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 2 percent in the month following CPI prints above 3.0 percent in over 70 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm a tightening labor market that supports corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio over 60-day forward windows across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly gross domestic product growth above trend reflects sustained aggregate demand expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns show no positive correlation with GDP growth rates over rolling 5-year evaluation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising consumer prices and falling industrial production indicates stagflationary supply shocks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Markets generate positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of periods exhibiting simultaneous price acceleration and output contraction.
